tech.title: World’s First Computer Virus Spreads Widely
tech.description: The Morris Worm, one of the earliest known computer worms, became the first to gain major public attention after infecting thousands of systems.

tech.title: SpaceX Successfully Tests Key Landing Technologies
tech.description: SpaceX completed tests that advanced its development of reusable rocket landing systems, critical to lowering spaceflight costs.

tech.title: cryptocurrency Market Hits $300 Billion
tech.description: The combined value of all cryptocurrencies surpassed $300 billion for the first time in history.

history.title: Ford Introduces the Moving Assembly Line
history.description: Henry Ford’s use of the first moving assembly line revolutionized manufacturing and drastically lowered production time.

history.title: Rosa Parks Refuses to Give Up Bus Seat
history.description: Rosa Parks’ arrest in Montgomery, Alabama sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott and became a key moment in the U.S. Civil Rights Movement.

history.title: Channel Tunnel Meets in the Middle
history.description: Workers digging from the UK and France broke through, creating the first land connection between Britain and mainland Europe in over 8,000 years.

world_events.title: Cold War Symbol Falls: East Germany Ends Travel Restrictions
world_events.description: East Germany officially ended restrictions on citizens traveling to the West, further accelerating the collapse of the Berlin Wall.

world_events.title: Lisbon Treaty Takes Effect
world_events.description: The Treaty of Lisbon, reforming the structure and leadership of the European Union, officially came into force.

world_events.title: UN Declares Global Climate Emergency Findings
world_events.description: The UN reported record levels of greenhouse gases and warned of increasing climate-related threats worldwide.

births.title: Woody Allen Born
births.description: American filmmaker, comedian, and writer known for his extensive filmography and unique comedic style.

births.title: Zoë Kravitz Born
births.description: American actress, singer, and model known for roles in ‘The Batman’ and the ‘Divergent’ series.

births.title: Sarah Silverman Born
births.description: American comedian and writer known for her satirical approach to comedy and social topics.

deaths.title: Aleister Crowley Dies
deaths.description: British occultist, writer, and mountaineer known for founding the religion of Thelema.

deaths.title: Chris Stamp Dies
deaths.description: British music producer and manager of iconic rock bands including The Who.

deaths.title: Lee Elder Dies
deaths.description: American professional golfer who broke racial barriers by becoming the first Black golfer to compete in the Masters Tournament.
